Documentation on using Jinja template inheritance to create base and child templates in Flask.

# Purpose
Template inheritance in Jinja allows developers to create a base "skeleton" template that contains common elements of a website and defines `blocks` that child templates can override. The base template, such as `layout.html`, provides a simple HTML structure with `block` tags that indicate sections where child templates can insert specific content. Child templates use the `{% extends %}` tag to inherit from the base template and can override the defined `blocks` to customize the content. The `{{ super() }}` function can be used within a block to include the content from the parent template's block, allowing for flexible and modular template design.
